title: Wizard of Oz: Novak Djokovic Shocks Jannik Sinner to Reach Australian Open Final
title_cn: 澳网奇迹：德约科维奇爆冷辛纳，晋级澳网决赛

paragraphs.en: Updated Jan. 30, 2026 10:46 am ET
paragraphs.cn: 更新于2026年1月30日美国东部时间上午10:46

paragraphs.en: Are you kidding me, tennis? What a delicious, unexpected thrill.
paragraphs.cn: 网球，你是在开玩笑吗？多么令人惊喜、出乎意料的刺激！

paragraphs.en: Nobody gave ancient Novak Djokovic much of a chance against Jannik Sinner in the Australian Open semifinals Friday—early Saturday in Melbourne, really—but the 38-year-old pulled off a five-set 3-6, 6-3, 4-6, 6-4, 6-4 stunner against a 24-year-old defending champ who was presumed to be too good and pulling away.
paragraphs.cn: 周五——实际上是墨尔本周六凌晨——没有人认为年迈的诺瓦克·德约科维奇在澳网半决赛中对阵扬尼克·辛纳会有多大机会，但这位38岁的老将以3-6、6-3、4-6、6-4、6-4的比分，在五盘大战中爆冷击败了24岁的卫冕冠军，后者被认为实力过强且势头正猛。

paragraphs.en: Djokovic did it with some old Djoko magic and verve. He was noisy, animated and aggressive. He hit winners he shouldn’t have tried and serves that looked as good as any serve he’d ever hit. He yelled at himself, the crowd, his box and into the void. Every so often he bent over and paused, looking like a man who’d just shoveled out his car.
paragraphs.cn: 德约科维奇凭借他那经典的“德约科维奇式”魔力和神韵做到了这一点。他吵闹、活泼且富有侵略性。他打出了本不该尝试的制胜分，发出了看起来是他职业生涯中最好的发球。他对自己、对观众、对他的包厢，甚至对着虚空大喊。他时不时地弯下腰停顿片刻，看起来就像一个刚铲完车道积雪的人。

paragraphs.en: Tired and graying, crows’ feet crinkling around his eyes, Djokovic summoned the vintage stuff and gave himself a chance.
paragraphs.cn: 疲惫而略显灰白，眼角布满鱼尾纹，德约科维奇唤起了他巅峰时期的状态，为自己赢得了一线生机。

paragraphs.en: Do you know how old Djokovic is? He’s almost as old as Jimmy Connors was when Connors did his geezer rally at the 1991 U.S. Open. Now he will meet world No. 1 Carlos Alcaraz Sunday for a chance at his 11th Australian Open title and 25th major overall.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道德约科维奇多大年纪了吗？他几乎和吉米·康纳斯在1991年美网“老将反击”时的年龄一样大。现在他将在周日迎战世界排名第一的卡洛斯·阿尔卡拉斯，争夺他的第11个澳网冠军和第25个大满贯头衔。

paragraphs.en: To paraphrase Jimbo: This is what we want. This is what we paid for.
paragraphs.cn: 借用吉姆博的话来说：这正是我们想要的。这正是我们花钱所求。

paragraphs.en: Djokovic doesn’t need this. Or does he? The Serbian is the most successful men’s tennis player to ever live, those 24 majors to his name, a winning record over both Rafael Nadal and Roger Federer, the most Masters 1000 titles ever, an Olympic gold, I don’t know what else there is to say.
paragraphs.cn: 德约科维奇并不需要这个。或者他需要吗？这位塞尔维亚人是史上最成功的男子网球运动员，拥有24个大满贯头衔，对拉斐尔·纳达尔和罗杰·费德勒都保持着胜绩，拥有史上最多的ATP1000大师赛冠军，一枚奥运金牌，我不知道还有什么可说的了。

paragraphs.en: We can fight over style points, and tennis artistry, and how Rod Laver would have done if given a private jet and synthetic string, but in terms of raw numbers, Novak is what a GOAT is supposed to be.
paragraphs.cn: 我们可以争论风格分、网球艺术性，以及如果给罗德·拉沃尔一架私人飞机和合成球线他会表现如何，但就纯粹的数字而言，诺瓦克就是“史上最佳”（GOAT）的代名词。

paragraphs.en: And he’s still out there, Ahab-like, one win away from a 25th that appeared to be disappearing in the rear view.
paragraphs.cn: 他仍然像亚哈船长一样执着地奋战着，距离第25个大满贯仅一步之遥，而这个目标曾一度似乎已从后视镜中消失。

paragraphs.en: Djokovic was lucky to be there. Very lucky—and he knew it. He got a walkover in the fourth round against an injured Jakub Mensik and in the quarterfinals he escaped a two-set deficit to Lorenzo Musetti when Musetti tweaked a groin muscle and couldn’t play any longer.
paragraphs.cn: 德约科维奇能走到这一步很幸运。非常幸运——他自己也知道。在第四轮，他因雅库布·门西克受伤而获得不战而胜；在四分之一决赛中，他面对洛伦佐·穆塞蒂曾两盘落后，但穆塞蒂腹股沟肌肉拉伤无法继续比赛，德约科维奇得以幸免。

paragraphs.en: “I was on my way home,” Djokovic admitted.
paragraphs.cn: “我当时正准备回家呢，”德约科维奇承认道。

paragraphs.en: His frightening reward: Sinner, the Italian hurricane and reigning back-to-back AO champion whom Djokovic hadn’t beaten in more than two years.
paragraphs.cn: 他所获得的惊人回报是：辛纳，这位意大利飓风，连续两届澳网卫冕冠军，德约科维奇已经两年多没有击败过他了。

paragraphs.en: Sinner plays like someone brought peak 2011 Djokovic into a shop and said: Make it even better. He is a ruthless, complete player, lethal from both sides of the baseline, able to scramble, extend points and hit drop shots when he needs to. He’s an absolute handful, which is how Djokovic historically prevailed.
paragraphs.cn: 辛纳的打法就像是有人把2011年巅峰时期的德约科维奇带到一家店里说：让它变得更好。他是一个冷酷无情、全面的球员，底线两边都极具杀伤力，能够奔跑、延长回合并在需要时打出放短球。他绝对是个难缠的对手，而德约科维奇历来就是这样取胜的。

paragraphs.en: Entering the match, Djokovic had dropped 10 straight sets to Sinner. Sinner soon made it 11. Djokovic turned up the risk, which can be desperate, but it worked, as he hit some big winners and serves and finally put a winning set on the board. Sinner responded to take the third, and Djokovic rallied with an early break to win the fourth.
paragraphs.cn: 赛前，德约科维奇已连续10盘输给辛纳。辛纳很快将这个数字变成了11。德约科维奇提高了风险，这可能很冒险，但奏效了，他打出了一些重要的制胜分和发球，最终赢下了一盘。辛纳随后赢得了第三盘，而德约科维奇则在第四盘开局就破发，最终赢得了该盘。

paragraphs.en: Earlier in the day, Alcaraz had outlasted third seed Alexander Zverev in a five-plus hour epic in which he overcame cramping and exhaustion to prevail in five sets. It was a gutty recovery by Alcaraz, but Zverev’s performance was also a bit of a flicker for men’s tennis.
paragraphs.cn: 当天早些时候，阿尔卡拉斯在一场耗时五个多小时的史诗级比赛中，克服了抽筋和疲惫，最终在五盘大战中战胜了三号种子亚历山大·兹维列夫。这是阿尔卡拉斯的一次顽强逆转，但兹维列夫的表现也让男子网球界出现了一丝希望。

paragraphs.en: Heading into the Open, there’d been a big howl about the distance between Alcaraz, Sinner and everyone else. In terms of points earned on the ATP Tour, there was a bigger gap between Sinner (11,500 points) and Zverev (5,105) than between Zverev and Ignacio Buse (627), the 100th ranked player in the world.
paragraphs.cn: 在澳网开赛前，人们曾对阿尔卡拉斯、辛纳与其他人之间的巨大差距大声疾呼。就ATP巡回赛积分而言，辛纳（11,500分）和兹维列夫（5,105分）之间的差距，甚至比兹维列夫和世界排名第100位的伊格纳西奥·布塞（627分）之间的差距还要大。

paragraphs.en: That’s not a gap—that’s a chasm. Alcaraz and Sinner desperately need a No. 3. Is it Zverev? Ben Shelton? Injured Jack Draper?
paragraphs.cn: 那不是差距——那是鸿沟。阿尔卡拉斯和辛纳迫切需要一个第三号人物。会是兹维列夫吗？本·谢尔顿？受伤的杰克·德雷珀？

paragraphs.en: If it’s going to be Djokovic, it’s only going to be for a bit.
paragraphs.cn: 如果会是德约科维奇，那也只会是暂时的。

paragraphs.en: He is still at it with nothing really left to prove. Djokovic has earned more money (nearly $200 million) winning tennis matches than anyone ever has. He leads a sumptuous life wherever he goes in the world. He could be relaxing at a cliff-side pool, jet-skiing behind a yacht, or sinking his teeth into a forbidden, gluten-rich blueberry muffin. He could put on 20 pounds and go on TV and tell us all what we’re doing wrong.
paragraphs.cn: 他仍在坚持，虽然真的没有什么需要证明的了。德约科维奇通过赢得网球比赛赚的钱（近2亿美元）比任何人都多。无论他走到世界何处，都过着奢华的生活。他本可以在悬崖边的泳池放松，在游艇后面玩水上摩托，或者大快朵颐一块被禁止的、富含麸质的蓝莓松饼。他可以增重20磅，然后上电视告诉我们所有人哪里做错了。

paragraphs.en: Instead he’s out there, on the other side of the world, in the arena, trying to get past the most fearsome players of the moment.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，他却身处世界的另一端，在赛场上，努力击败当下最可怕的球员。

paragraphs.en: Now Novak Djokovic will try to get one more. I’ll say it again: what a delicious, unexpected thrill.
paragraphs.cn: 现在诺瓦克·德约科维奇将努力再争取一个冠军。我再说一遍：多么令人惊喜、出乎意料的刺激！

paragraphs.en: Appeared in the January 31, 2026, print edition as 'Wizard of Oz: Djokovic Advances To Another Australian Open Final'.
paragraphs.cn: 刊登于2026年1月31日印刷版，标题为“澳网奇迹：德约科维奇晋级又一澳网决赛”。

paragraphs.en: Jason Gay is The Wall Street Journal’s sports columnist. In 2024, Jason's sports column was awarded first place by the National Society of Newspaper Columnists. He was named Sports Columnist of the Year by the Society of Professional Journalists in 2010, 2016 and 2019. He is author of the essay book "I Wouldn't Do That If I Were Me," published in 2022, and his 2015 bestseller “Little Victories” was a finalist for the Thurber Prize for American Humor. Write to Jason at jason.gay@wsj.com, and follow him on Twitter @jasongay. Sign up to receive email alerts from Jason Gay.
paragraphs.cn: 杰森·盖伊是《华尔街日报》的体育专栏作家。2024年，杰森的体育专栏荣获美国报纸专栏作家协会一等奖。他曾于2010年、2016年和2019年被专业记者协会评为年度体育专栏作家。他是2022年出版的散文集《如果我是我，我就不会那么做》的作者，他2015年的畅销书《小胜利》曾入围瑟伯美国幽默奖决赛。请通过jason.gay@wsj.com联系杰森，并在Twitter上关注他@jasongay。注册接收杰森·盖伊的电子邮件提醒。

vocabulary.meaning_en: An extremely surprising or impressive event or thing.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 令人震惊的事件；爆冷门
vocabulary.example: the 38-year-old pulled off a five-set 3-6, 6-3, 4-6, 6-4, 6-4 stunner against a 24-year-old defending champ who was presumed to be too good and pulling away.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这位38岁的老将以3-6、6-3、4-6、6-4、6-4的比分，在五盘大战中爆冷击败了24岁的卫冕冠军，后者被认为实力过强且势头正猛。

vocabulary.example: Djokovic did it with some old Djoko magic and verve.
vocabulary.example_cn: 德约科维奇凭借他那经典的“德约科维奇式”魔力和神韵做到了这一点。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To call upon (a quality, feeling, or action) for a particular purpose.
vocabulary.example: Tired and graying, crows’ feet crinkling around his eyes, Djokovic summoned the vintage stuff and gave himself a chance.
vocabulary.example_cn: 疲惫而略显灰白，眼角布满鱼尾纹，德约科维奇唤起了他巅峰时期的状态，为自己赢得了一线生机。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To express the meaning of (something written or spoken) using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity.
vocabulary.example: To paraphrase Jimbo: This is what we want. This is what we paid for.
vocabulary.example_cn: 借用吉姆博的话来说：这正是我们想要的。这正是我们花钱所求。

vocabulary.example: We can fight over style points, and tennis artistry, and how Rod Laver would have done if given a private jet and synthetic string
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们可以争论风格分、网球艺术性，以及如果给罗德·拉沃尔一架私人飞机和合成球线他会表现如何。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A profound difference between people, viewpoints, feelings, etc.
vocabulary.example: That’s not a gap—that’s a chasm.
He leads a sumptuous life wherever he goes in the world.
vocabulary.example_cn: 无论他走到世界何处，都过着奢华的生活。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To prove more powerful or superior; to be victorious.
vocabulary.example: Alcaraz had outlasted third seed Alexander Zverev in a five-plus hour epic in which he overcame cramping and exhaustion to prevail in five sets.
vocabulary.example_cn: 阿尔卡拉斯在一场耗时五个多小时的史诗级比赛中，克服了抽筋和疲惫，最终在五盘大战中战胜了三号种子亚历山大·兹维列夫。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The amount by which something, especially a sum of money, is too small; a disadvantage or shortage.
vocabulary.example: in the quarterfinals he escaped a two-set deficit to Lorenzo Musetti when Musetti tweaked a groin muscle and couldn’t play any longer.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在四分之一决赛中，他面对洛伦佐·穆塞蒂曾两盘落后，但穆塞蒂腹股沟肌肉拉伤无法继续比赛，德约科维奇得以幸免。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Full of life or excitement; lively.
vocabulary.example: He was noisy, animated and aggressive.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他吵闹、活泼且富有侵略性。
